employee_request.title: Public Health Nurse
employee_request.intermittent_or_reduced_work_schedule_de:
I am requesting an initial continuous leave followed by a short period of reduced schedule, based on my orthopedic surgeon’s recommendations and the anticipated physical therapy plan.

1. Continuous leave: From 2025-01-06 through approximately 2025-03-02, I anticipate being completely off work. During the first 2–3 weeks after surgery I will be largely non-weight-bearing on my right knee, on prescription pain medication that makes driving unsafe, and attending frequent medical and physical therapy appointments. My job as a public health nurse often requires driving to home visits in rural areas, climbing stairs in client homes, and spending long periods standing at community clinics, all of which I will not be able to do safely or reliably during this period.

2. Transition to reduced schedule: Starting around 2025-03-03 (depending on my doctor’s clearance), I may be able to return on a reduced schedule of approximately 4–5 hours per day, 3 days per week, for 3–4 weeks. During this time I would focus on desk-based tasks such as charting, phone outreach, data entry, and planning work that does not require driving to client homes or prolonged standing. I will still be attending physical therapy 2–3 times per week in the afternoons, so I would request the ability to schedule my work hours earlier in the day (for example, 8:30 a.m. to 12:30 or 1:30 p.m.) on days I am in the office.

3. Intermittent medical appointments: Even after I return to full duty, I anticipate occasional follow-up appointments with my orthopedic surgeon and physical therapist (likely monthly) for the rest of the year. I would request flexibility to use intermittent FMLA for those specific medical visits and recovery time as needed, generally 1–2 hours per appointment.

I understand that the exact timing of the transition from continuous leave to reduced schedule may change depending on my recovery and the written guidance from my doctor and physical therapist. I will keep my supervisor and HR informed and will provide updated medical documentation if the schedule needs to be adjusted.
